Explanation: This report presents a specialized workflow for detecting instrument artifacts in chromatography data using Asymmetric Least Squares baseline correction and volume-based rolling Z-score analysis with cross-sensor correlation logic. Strengths include clear methodology description, appropriate technical approach for the domain, and well-structured presentation with supporting visualizations and code. However, several limitations reduce the overall quality: (1) The finding of zero instrument artifacts across 11 runs is remarkable and concerning—lack of discussion about detection sensitivity and false-negative rates; (2) limited validation evidence; only two representative runs visualized without statistical justification; (3) methodology lacks crucial details on parameter selection (e.g., why λ≈10⁶⁻¹⁰⁷, p≈2×10⁻⁴⁻²×10⁻⁵ for AsLS) and no sensitivity analysis; (4) no comparison with alternative methods or ground truth validation; (5) figures are present but minimally integrated into analysis; (6) missing discussion of limitations, edge cases, or failure modes. The code is comprehensive but somewhat difficult to follow due to complexity. Overall, the work is technically competent but would benefit from deeper critical analysis and more rigorous validation of the null result.
